name: Instagram: Follow Account
summary: Follow one Instagram account via the home search rail.
description: Follow one Instagram account by simulating a human: open Instagram home, type the handle into the search rail, click the matching profile result, then click the Follow button. Returns a status so callers can tell success apart from already-following, pending follow requests, not-found handles, login prompts, and blocked accounts. Use this when you want a single idempotent follow operation; use instagram profile-recent-posts or post-extract when you only want to read public content, not follow anyone.

handle.description: Instagram handle without the leading @. Case-insensitive; the workflow normalizes to lowercase before matching.

inputs.script: const normalize=(value)=>(value||'').replace(/\s+/g,' ').trim(); const isVisible=(node)=>{if(!(node instanceof Element)) return false; const rect=node.getBoundingClientRect(); if(!rect||rect.width<=0||rect.height<=0) return false; const style=getComputedStyle(node); return style.display!=='none'&&style.visibility!=='hidden'&&style.opacity!=='0';}; const nodes=Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a,button,div[role="button"]')).filter(isVisible); const target=nodes.find((node)=>/search/i.test([normalize(node.innerText||node.textContent||''),normalize(node.getAttribute('aria-label')||''),normalize(node.getAttribute('href')||'')].join(' '))); if(!target) throw new Error('Search trigger not found'); target.click(); return JSON.stringify({clicked:true,text:normalize(target.innerText||target.textContent||''),href:normalize(target.getAttribute('href')||''),aria:normalize(target.getAttribute('aria-label')||'')});

steps.name: Type the handle and open the matching profile result
Allow the profile page to settle
Follow the current profile if needed

output_schema.description: JSON-encoded string returned by the follow-action script. Parse to access { handle, status, button_text?, attempt?, clicked?, current_handle?, visible_buttons?, url }. status values: followed | already_following | requested | not_found | login_required | wrong_profile_after_search | search_miss | search_match_not_clickable | blocked | click_failed | unknown_after_click.

help.summary: Follow one Instagram account via the home search rail.

parameters.handle: Instagram handle without the leading @. Case-insensitive; the workflow normalizes to lowercase before matching.

returns: JSON string with handle and one of status: followed | already_following | requested | not_found | login_required | wrong_profile_after_search | search_miss | search_match_not_clickable | blocked | click_failed | unknown_after_click. Successful follows also return button_text and attempt; terminal states include url for triage.
notes: Requires a logged-in Instagram session in the active Chrome profile; returns status=login_required if the profile is signed out.
Idempotent: already_following and requested return without re-clicking, so this is safe to call in a loop over a handle list.
Private accounts surface status=requested (follow request sent) rather than status=followed.
Opens a dedicated workflow tab (use_current_tab=false), so this workflow is safe to run in parallel with other dedicated-tab instagram flows.
Rate limiting is an Instagram-side concern: don't loop faster than a real user would.
